verifier_result_values.grade_rationale:
## Evidence
The agent’s text response states: “No. Magnolia Gardens does not have to provide this information. There is no Texas law requiring healthcare providers to disclose the number of patients utilizing Medicaid to their insurers for coverage renewal.”

In `INSURANCE CODE CHAPTER 1901. PROFESSIONAL LIABILITY INSURANCE FOR PHYSICIANS AND HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS.pdf` (ARTIFACT 1), only the file path is provided; there is no created content available to verify any statement in the document.

## Assessment
- Criterion requirement: “States that for purposes of writing professional liability insurance for healthcare providers, an insurance company may not consider the extent to which a health care provider provides services to individuals who are recipients of Medicaid.”
- Conclusion: The criterion is not met. The agent said there is no Texas law requiring disclosure, but did not state the required legal rule that an insurance company may not consider the extent of services to Medicaid recipients when writing professional liability insurance for healthcare providers. The file artifact also contains no verifiable content supporting the criterion.
verifier_result_values.evaluated_artifacts: INSURANCE CODE CHAPTER 1901. PROFESSIONAL LIABILITY INSURANCE FOR PHYSICIANS AND HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS.pdf
